food_subgroup: Pizza-based combo meal with side dish and beverage

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general pizza food class (FOODON_00002473). The full combo meal including side and drink has no exact ontology match; closest_parent used for the pizza component.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers pizza as a food category. The combo meal format (pizza + side + drink) is not individually coded; closest_parent applied to the primary pizza component.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226559009 represents pizza as a food concept. The full meal set is not individually coded; closest_parent used.

serving.description: 1 pizza meal set (pizza slice or personal pizza + side + drink)
serving.natural_language_portion: One typical pizza meal set consisting of a personal pizza or multiple slices (approximately 350 g), a side dish such as fries or coleslaw (approximately 100 g), and a soft drink or beverage (approximately 100 g equivalent), totalling approximately 550 g.

nutrient_source.title: Pizza, cheese, regular crust (USDA SR Legacy, NDB 21299) combined with composite meal estimation for side and beverage components
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact nutritional record exists for this specific combo meal from an approved primary source. Nutrient values are estimated using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for cheese pizza (regular crust) as the primary component, representing approximately 64% of total meal weight. Side dish (fries, ~18% weight) and beverage (~18% weight) components are estimated from USDA SR Legacy analogues. Values are expressed per 100 g of the composite meal. This is a medium-confidence estimate. HPB Singapore Foodo does not carry a direct record for this commercial pizza combo meal.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of pizza and pizza-based meals — analogous estimate based on 'Pizza, Super Supreme, Pizza Hut' (GI = 36) and 'Pizza, cheese' (GI = 60) from the University of Sydney GI Database; composite meal GI estimated at 60 reflecting refined wheat crust dominance and mixed macronutrient profile
gi_source.source_match_notes: The University of Sydney GI Database lists cheese pizza at GI 60 and Pizza Hut Super Supreme at GI 36. For a standard commercial cheese/topping pizza with regular wheat crust, GI 60 is used as the representative value. The composite meal GI is estimated at 60 accounting for the pizza as the dominant glycemic component. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI x carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (60 x 148.5) / 100 = 89.1, where carbohydrate per 550 g serving = 27.0 x 5.5 = 148.5 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~480 mg per 100 g; ~2640 mg per full meal set)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this meal as a single serving may provide more than the recommended daily sodium intake of 2000 mg; request reduced-sodium toppings and avoid adding extra salt or dipping sauces.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load per serving (~89 GL units) driven by refined wheat crust and total carbohydrate content
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this meal only occasionally and in reduced portions, as the high glycemic load from the refined-flour pizza base combined with the side dish and sweetened beverage can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; choosing a thin-crust option and replacing the sugary drink with water may help mitigate the glycemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~3.8 g per 100 g) and sodium (~480 mg per 100 g) from cheese, processed meat toppings, and fried side components
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit this meal to occasional consumption, selecting vegetable-topped pizza with reduced cheese and avoiding fried side dishes to reduce saturated fat and sodium intake in line with heart-healthy dietary guidelines.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Pizza Meal Set (Pizza with Side and Drink) is a commercial combo meal offered by pizza restaurants in Singapore, notably by Now Pizza at Funan mall. The set typically comprises a personal pizza or multiple slices featuring a refined wheat-flour crust topped with tomato sauce, mozzarella cheese, and a choice of toppings (such as pepperoni, chicken, vegetables, or seafood), paired with a side dish (commonly french fries, coleslaw, or garlic bread) and a beverage (typically a soft drink or juice). The pizza is baked in a commercial oven, yielding a crisp-edged crust with a soft, chewy interior and a rich, savoury topping layer. The overall meal is energy-dense, providing approximately 215 kcal per 100 g and roughly 1180 kcal for a full 550 g set. The meal is high in refined carbohydrates (27 g per 100 g), moderate in protein (8.2 g per 100 g), and contains notable saturated fat (3.8 g per 100 g) and sodium (480 mg per 100 g). The glycemic index of the pizza component is estimated at 60 (medium GI), and the glycemic load per full serving is high at approximately 89 GL units, making this meal a significant glycemic challenge for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The high sodium content (~2640 mg per full meal) is a concern for individuals with hypertension. From a cardiovascular perspective, the saturated fat from cheese and processed meat toppings warrants moderation. This meal is widely consumed as a casual dining or delivery option in Singapore and is representative of the growing Western fast-casual food segment in the city-state.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a pizza meal set?
Sodium content of pizza combo meal
Is pizza meal high in fat?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional record for this specific commercial pizza combo meal exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central). Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy cheese pizza data as the primary component, with composite meal adjustments for side and beverage. GI is estimated from the closest analogous pizza entries in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium due to the composite and variable nature of the meal.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (cheese pizza, regular crust) as the dominant component (~64% of meal weight), with side dish and beverage components estimated from USDA SR Legacy analogues. Values represent a composite per-100 g estimate for the full meal set. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy pizza source entry. 3. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy pizza source entry. 4. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the composite meal estimation source. 5. GI estimated at 60 based on University of Sydney GI Database analogous pizza entries (cheese pizza GI 60). Glycemic load per serving = (60 x 148.5) / 100 = 89.1, where 148.5 g carbohydrate is derived from 27.0 g per 100 g x 550 g serving. 6. The meal composition is variable depending on pizza topping selection, side dish choice, and beverage selection; actual nutritional values may differ significantly from estimates. 7. Halal status is not confirmed for Now Pizza - Funan; is_halal set to false pending verification. 8. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, and water_g are estimated from USDA SR Legacy pizza data and are approximate.

q1_food_identity.notes: Canonical English food name 'Pizza Meal Set (Pizza with Side and Drink)' is precise and non-promotional. Mandatory local-language names present for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi as required for Singapore-served Western food with multilingual consumer base.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: All four core macros (energy_kcal 215.0, protein_g 8.2, fat_g 8.5, carbohydrate_g 27.0) are present, numeric, and non-zero. Source is USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (approved secondary global source), clearly named with adaptation notes explaining composite meal estimation.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (1.8), sodium_mg (480.0), saturated_fat_g (3.8), and sugar_g (3.5) are all present as numeric values and are plausible for a pizza-based combo meal.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index = 60 (medium, correctly classified as 56-69 range), gi_category = 'medium', glycemic_load_per_serving = 89.1, gi_source present and references University of Sydney GI Database analogous pizza entries. GI threshold correctly applied: 60 is medium (56-69).

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn FOODON_00002473 (pizza food), FoodEx2 A00LC (pizza), and SNOMED CT 226559009 (pizza food) all used as closest_parent with honest gap notes explaining that the full combo meal has no exact ontology code. All match_type values are from the allowed set: exact, closest_parent, none.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is well over 200 characters (~1450 characters), explains what the food is, preparation method, taste/texture, cultural context in Singapore, clinical relevance for diabetes/hypertension/CVD, and key nutritional profile.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension (high concern, sodium), Type 2 Diabetes (high concern, glycemic load), Cardiovascular Disease (moderate concern, saturated fat and sodium). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence. human_condition_name 'Type 2 Diabetes' used correctly.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: aliases list contains 14 entries covering English variants, Chinese simplified, Chinese traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi, and romanized forms. aliases_by_language is fully consistent with flat aliases list — every string in aliases_by_language verified to appear verbatim in flat aliases array. Comprehensive coverage of alternate English names, regional variants, and local-language forms.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 2: Previous iteration produced invalid JSON (empty output from gemini engine). Rebuilt complete valid JSON from scratch.

Iteration 3: Verified all hard gates pass. Confirmed aliases_by_language and flat aliases are perfectly consistent. Confirmed all -1 sentinel values documented in notes. Confirmed GI category 'medium' correctly applied for GI=60. Confirmed ontology match_type values use only allowed strings. Confirmed zero-macro justification not needed as no core macros are zero. Confirmed multilingual naming complete for Singapore-served food.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — All 6 hard gates pass and all 8 scoring criteria pass (8/8). The record provides a well-sourced composite nutritional estimate for a commercial pizza combo meal using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as the approved primary source, with honest adaptation notes. GI is estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous pizza entries. Ontology uses closest_parent codes with honest gap notes. Multilingual naming covers Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi as required. All sentinel -1 values are documented. Health context covers all three required conditions with specific clinical guidance. LLM description exceeds 200 characters with substantive content.
